title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a report on Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Public Standards, outlining its stance and actions regarding the development and use of AI. The exact details of the response are not specified in the title, but it implies the government's reaction to recommendations made by a committee.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: The document provides a report and assessment on how copyright law applies to the creation of artificial intelligence. It explores the impact on copyright works being used in AI system development.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education is creating a policy around the use of generative AI in educational settings, focusing on its impact and potential benefits. This policy aims to provide guidance for educators, students, and institutions in safely and effectively incorporating generative AI into education.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines the government's plan for integrating artificial intelligence in transportation systems. It aims to balance the benefits of AI with potential risks and consequences, by working closely with the transport sector.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report discusses the use of copyrighted materials in developing artificial intelligence (AI) systems. It focuses on progress made under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations on how to regulate artificial intelligence (AI) used in the medical field to ensure safety and effectiveness. The aim is to establish clear guidelines for developing and using AI-powered medical devices.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual is creating a policy to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in qualification assessments, responding to government requests. The goal is to ensure AI is used fairly and doesn't compromise the integrity of qualifications.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to the House of Lords Select Committee's report on Artificial Intelligence, acknowledging the importance of this technology in the country. The government is expected to take steps to address concerns and implement recommendations from the report to ensure the safe development and use of AI in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference for a committee reviewing artificial intelligence and its impact on public standards. The terms of reference will guide the committee's examination of AI's effects on societal norms and expectations.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has submitted evidence to a parliamentary inquiry on how artificial intelligence should be governed. The submission provides written evidence to inform the committee's conclusions on AI governance.
